design_philosophy.core_loop: Research technologies, expand colonies, manage faction ideologies, compete for planetary dominance through military, diplomatic, economic, or transcendence victories
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Ideological conflict between seven human factions with opposing philosophical visions for humanity's future, compounded by Planet's emerging sentience as an adaptive adversary
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a faction of colonists to establish humanity's foothold on an alien world, shape civilization according to your ideological beliefs, and achieve victory through chosen means (conquest, diplomacy, science, or transcendence)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game explicitly models ideology as mechanical advantage: each faction's social engineering paradigm directly affects gameplay (production, morale, economy). Rejects 'neutral' colonization—player must commit to a philosophical vision (militarism, theocracy, capitalism, communism, environmentalism, etc.) that determines available unit types, diplomatic relationships, and victory paths. Planet itself becomes an ideological actor, not just terrain. The game treats competing political philosophies as genuinely incompatible endpoints, not negotiable differences.

difficulty.checkpointing: Turn-based design allows save/load, but single-game persistence creates meaningful failure states
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple difficulty levels implied by faction selection; player can customize opponent AI and faction capabilities; open-ended victory conditions allow players to pursue suited strategies

progression.upgrade_system: Technology tree with branching paths; social engineering allows rapid tactical shifts in faction values; unit customization enables adaptation to evolving threats
progression.unlock_mechanism: Technologies unlock units, facilities, diplomatic options, and secret projects; faction-specific units locked to ideological commitments; Planet's sentience progressively manifests as game advances, creating emergent obstacles
replayability_hooks: Seven distinct factions with unique unit rosters and mechanical advantages
Multiple victory conditions (military conquest, diplomatic council, economic ascendancy, transcendence/planetmind)
Procedural planet generation and faction opponent selection
Social engineering system allows radical mid-game strategy pivots
Alien Crossfire adds five additional human factions and two non-human Progenitor factions
Custom unit design system enables player-authored faction strategies

world_building_techniques: Voiced faction leader monologues delivered upon technology discovery, revealing personal backstories and ideological rationales
In-game Datalinks provide lore exposition and world context
Comm-link 'photographs' establish character development through visual storytelling (portraits, family moments, spacewalk memories)
Planet itself communicates through environmental phenomena and manifests narrative agency as sentience awakens
Scientific tech descriptions embed worldbuilding (references to Earth pre-departure, colonial struggles, alien discoveries)
Alien Crossfire narrative layer: Progenitor experiments and Tau Ceti Flowering history explains systemic stakes

boss_design.philosophy: Planet itself evolves as primary antagonist: native alien life becomes increasingly sophisticated and aggressive as game progresses; no traditional boss encounters, but rival faction leaders function as persistent strategic opponents with adaptive AI and ideological commitment to specific victory paths
notable_bosses: Planet's Manifold (emergent planetary sentience that generates native lifeforms and environmental hazards)
Human Hive (Chairman Yang—authoritarian faction that excels at unit production and population control)
Spartan Federation (Colonel Santiago—militarist faction with superior combat units)
University of Planet (Akademician Zakharov—technologist pursuing scientific victory)
Progenitor Manifold Caretakers and Usurpers (Alien Crossfire expansion—non-human factions with advanced technology)

faction_indicators: Human Hive (authoritarian collectivism, eugenics, hive-mind aspiration)
University of Planet (scientific rationalism, technological transcendence)
Spartan Federation (military discipline, eugenic warrior culture)
danger_signals: Authoritarian factions (Human Hive, Spartan Federation) pursue militaristic dominance and population control
Isolationist ideologies prevent diplomatic cooperation and increase conflict probability
Transcendence victory conditions (planetary mind merger) represent existential threat to human autonomy
Progenitor factions in Alien Crossfire introduce post-human experimentation agendas
Ecological collapse mechanics reward or punish faction environmental policies
Espionage and intrigue systems enable covert dominance
environmental_storytelling: Rocky planet terrain and alien fungal forests establish extraterrestrial colonization struggle
Native lifeform evolution reflects player ecological decisions
Faction bases architecturally reflect ideological identity (military fortifications, religious monuments, corporate arcologies, research campuses)
Monoliths and Progenitor artifacts scattered across landscape signal ancient experimentation history
Climate zone shifts caused by industrial development show human impact on planetary systems
Secret projects (orbital stations, psych warfare networks, planetary mind) visualize faction aspirations
